hardwareModule.title: Hardware Module - Simplified View
hardwareModule.overview: Shows how hardware requests flow from Java → JNI → Native → Hardware

layer2_jni.name: JNI Bridge Layer
layer2_jni.note: All JNI calls are BLOCKING while native code executes

handlers.Java_...discoverProbes(): Enumerate USB devices → Query vendor/product/serial → Return list
handlers.Java_...connect(): Open USB handle → Load chipset driver → Init protocol → Ready
handlers.Java_...sendCommand(): Parse command → Route to chipset driver → Get response
handlers.Java_...readMemory(): Route to chipset driver → Read target address → Return bytes
handlers.Java_...writeMemory(): Route to chipset driver → Write target address → Verify

functions: s32k148_init() - Setup SWD pins and negotiate speed
s32k148_send_command() - Send CMM command via SWD bitbang
mpc5777m_init() - Setup JTAG pins, TAP reset, sync cores
mpc5777m_send_command() - Send CMM via JTAG IR/DR

steps: 2. HardwareService queues in thread pool, returns CompletableFuture
6. Build HardwareInfo array from USB device list
7. Return jobjectArray back across JNI boundary
9. Frontend callback receives probe list, updates UI
5. Route to active chipset driver (S32K148 or MPC5777M)
7. Bitbang SWIO/SWCK pins (SWD) or toggle JTAG pins (JTAG)
10. Return CommandResult back to Java via JNI

timing.note: Governed by USB/debug protocol speed, not CPU

concurrency.JNI Bridge: Blocking - Java thread waits for native code

errorFlow.native: chipset driver returns error code (AITT_OK, AITT_ERROR_*)
errorFlow.jni: Native function checks code, throws NativeException if error
errorFlow.java: HardwareService catches exception, logs, fires ErrorEvent
errorFlow.frontend: Frontend receives ErrorEvent or failed CompletableFuture, shows error dialog

key_insights: All JNI calls block the calling thread - that's why async/thread pool wrapper exists
CompletableFuture keeps UI responsive even though JNI blocks
Chipset drivers are the actual hardware protocol handlers
S32K148 uses SWD (2-pin protocol), MPC5777M uses JTAG (4-pin protocol)
Each target has its own driver with init/send/read/write functions
USB enumeration finds available debug probes
Debug probes bridge USB (host) to SWD/JTAG (target)
